# **CHAPTER 1** {#chapter-1}

# **THE PROBLEM AND ITS BACKGROUND** {#the-problem-and-its-background}

## **1.1 Background of the Study** {#1.1-background-of-the-study}

Over the years, technology has significantly transformed the way people manage tasks and communicate. At the same time, rapid urbanization has increased the demand for rental properties, particularly multi-unit residential buildings such as apartments, dormitories, and boarding houses. In today's digital age, access to information and the internet has become a foundation for personal, economic, and political advancement, influencing how businesses and services operate. As a result, many organizations now rely on technology to improve efficiency, increase productivity, and deliver services that meet evolving user expectations. In the rental housing sector, the growing number of tenants and rental units has introduced significant operational challenges for landlords in organizing property information, maintaining tenant records, monitoring rental payments, and addressing maintenance concerns efficiently. Meanwhile, tenants increasingly expect transparency, timely communication, and convenient access to their rental information throughout the duration of their stay.

This research is set within the larger urban context of Valenzuela City, a premier industrial and residential hub that serves a large, mobile population of workers, students, and families. The study focuses on high-density rental corridors that stretch from the commercial and educational hubs of Marulas and Malinta to the essential industrial zones of Canumay and Maysan. Due to its strategic location near major expressways, manufacturing companies, and numerous institutions, the city holds a broad inventory of rental buildings, including large-scale flats, worker dormitories, and conventional boarding houses. As the demand for housing across these barangays continues to rise, landlords face growing pressure to manage multiple units and diverse tenant profiles simultaneously. Despite Valenzuela's rapid economic and industrial development, many rental operators in these key neighborhoods continue to rely on fragmented, manual methods, approaches that are becoming increasingly insufficient to handle the operational demands of a modernizing city.

Several systemic challenges have emerged within the rental housing sector across the diverse districts of Valenzuela City. Landlords in high-density areas ranging from the industrial zones of Canumay and Maysan to the residential centers of Marulas and Malinta frequently encounter difficulties in organizing tenant data, maintaining lease records, and monitoring rental payments due to the absence of a centralized, integrated system. The continuous reliance on manual record-keeping across multiple properties raises the risk of data inconsistencies, inaccuracies, and miscommunication, which frequently leads to disputes and operational bottlenecks. Tenants, on the other hand, report ongoing concerns such as unclear payment histories, limited visibility into their lease details, and delayed responses to maintenance complaints. These inefficiencies not only hinder the scalability of local rental enterprises, but also reduce the overall quality of the landlord-tenant relationship within Valenzuela's diverse renter community.

To address these concerns, the development of iReside, an Integrated Property and Tenant Management Platform, is proposed as a focused, private digital solution for legally operating dormitories, apartments, and boarding houses throughout Valenzuela City. Rather than functioning as a public-facing rental marketplace, iReside is designed as an exclusive, invitation-based system centered on the operational needs of property owners. Following a model similar to that of communication platforms where a host creates a private workspace and grants access to members, iReside allows each landlord to independently establish and manage their own set of properties, with each property serving as a self-contained, isolated environment for its respective tenants. Tenants gain access only through direct enrollment by their landlord, ensuring that all data remains private and confined within each property's boundaries.

Within this closed ecosystem, iReside empowers landlords to digitally oversee tenant records, visualize occupancy through a unit map, and track financial histories with real-time situational awareness. A key innovation of the platform is the incorporation of a Supportive Artificial Intelligence (AI) Utility; by leveraging text and image-based analysis, this AI component will triage maintenance reports and surface recurring patterns in tenant data to provide actionable insights for property owners. For tenants, the platform offers direct access to digital receipts, building announcements, and an automated channel for reporting concerns, all within the scope of their enrolled property. By consolidating property administration into a structured, role-based digital environment, iReside seeks to modernize the rental management ecosystem of Valenzuela City, ensuring transparency, operational efficiency, and a digitally integrated future for local landlords and their communities.

## **1.1.2 Project Context** {#1.1.2-project-context}

The increasing demand for rental housing in Valenzuela City's key areas, including the high-density residential zones of Marulas and Malinta as well as the industrial hubs of Canumay and Maysan, highlights the critical need for a modernized, technology-driven approach to property management. Valenzuela, a highly urbanized city, holds a substantial inventory of dormitories, apartments, and boarding houses that serve a diverse population of factory workers, students, and families. Despite this expansion, the local rental landscape remains dominated by fragmented, manual operations. Landlords across these barangays experience persistent challenges in record-keeping, payment monitoring, and maintenance management, while tenants face difficulties with communication and access to their own rental information. These inefficiencies underscore a crucial gap between the city's rapid industrial growth and its continued reliance on outdated management systems, a gap that demands an integrated digital solution capable of consolidating operations and ensuring accountability across the rental community.

To address this gap, the project introduces iReside, a private, invitation-based property and tenant management platform delivered through a web-based architecture. iReside operates on a closed, role-based model analogous to how dedicated communication platforms allow a host to create isolated private workspaces for their members. In iReside, each landlord independently registers and manages their own set of properties, and each property serves as a self-contained digital environment accessible only to the tenants enrolled within it. There is no public listing, no unit discovery, and no tenant self-registration; access is granted exclusively through the landlord, ensuring that all data remains private and scoped to each property's boundaries.

The platform enforces this structure through a tri-tier Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) system:

* Administrators oversee platform-wide operations, managing user registrations, reviewing landlord applications, and ensuring system integrity across all properties.  
* Landlords hold full operational control within their properties, managing tenant records, monitoring occupancy and finances, and overseeing maintenance across as many properties as they own.  
* Tenants interact exclusively within their enrolled property, with access to their lease details, payment history, maintenance requests, and communication channels.

A core technical feature of the platform is the unit map, a drag-and-drop visual builder that enables landlords to digitize their physical property layouts into an interactive map of floors, wings, and units. This visual layer provides real-time situational awareness, allowing landlords to instantly assess occupancy status, monitor payment standing, and flag active maintenance concerns, all from a single interface.

The platform is further enhanced by the iRis AI Assistant, a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)-powered chatbot built on Groq's Llama 3.1 inference engine. For tenants, iRis provides context-aware, natural language responses about their lease, amenities, and building rules. On the administrative side, the AI component assists in triaging maintenance reports, identifying urgency patterns, and surfacing actionable insights from tenant and payment data, reducing cognitive load for property owners while maintaining operational precision.

Beyond management tools, iReside includes a Community Hub that facilitates structured engagement within each property. Landlords can publish announcements, utility alerts, and pinned notices, while tenants can participate in community discussions and polls, all moderated through a landlord-controlled approval queue to preserve a respectful and organized environment.

By unifying property administration, tenant communication, financial tracking, and AI-assisted decision-making into a single, role-governed digital environment, iReside provides a scalable and secure foundation for modernizing rental management across Valenzuela City, establishing transparency and operational efficiency as the new standard for local landlords and their communities.

## **1.2 Problem Statement** {#1.2-problem-statement}

The management of residential properties in Valenzuela City remains largely dependent on traditional, manual ledgers and disconnected digital records, which fail to provide the real-time operational visibility and structured coordination required for modern property oversight. This fragmented approach forces landlords to juggle separate tools for tracking occupancy, maintenance, and financial transactions, leading to data overlaps, delayed responses to tenant concerns, and a significant lack of transparency that often erodes trust and compliance with local rental standards. Furthermore, the absence of an integrated platform means critical data, such as rental payments, lease updates, and maintenance reports are rarely synchronized in real-time, while the current lack of AI-driven tools limits the ability to automate tenant support and triage urgent property issues. To address these administrative inefficiencies and scalability challenges, this study proposes the development of iReside, a web-based, private property and tenant management system that centralizes administration through unit map, AI-powered assistance, and a secure role-based architecture, aimed at establishing a more accountable and digitally integrated rental management experience across the city's residential sectors.

Specifically, this study investigates:

1. What are the essential functional requirements and system components needed to develop an integrated property and tenant management platform for residential buildings in Valenzuela City?  
2. In what ways does the absence of a centralized data architecture and real-time synchronization in traditional systems result in data fragmentation and delayed information updates for both landlords and tenants?  
3. How can the integration of a unit map and an AI-powered assistant automate maintenance tracking and provide real-time updates on property status?  
4. How can the implementation of an automated digital ledger and secure data access protocols enhance the precision of financial tracking and the overall reliability of property documentation?  
5. To what extent does the iReside system meet international technical standards based on the ISO/IEC 25010 Software Quality Model?

### **1.2.1 General Objectives** {#1.2.1-general-objectives}

The primary goal of this research is to design and create iReside: Property and Tenant Services Management System, a web-based centralized property hub command center for Valenzuela City landlords that includes a dedicated mobile-responsive interface for their tenants. The unit map attempts to consolidate administrative activities such as unit monitoring, financial tracking, and maintenance triage, while tenants will have access to a Managed-Access Portal for localized services. By using a strict role-based access architecture, the platform ensures that resident participation is directly related to their lease status, resulting in a secure and professionally managed digital environment for multi-unit apartments.

### **1.2.2 Specific Objectives** {#1.2.2-specific-objectives}

1\. To develop and evaluate iReside: A Modern Property and Tenant Services Platform with the following features for different user roles:

> [!NOTE]
> **Deployment-Based Model**: iReside follows an exclusive deployment model for landlords. The development team deploys and hands over independent property instances to landlords who become independent administrators of their own operational environments. There is no centralized institutional operator—each landlord receives their own independently operated deployment.

    a. Enable Super Administrators (Development Team) to manage:

        i. View real-time KPIs on the Live Metrics Dashboard (Total Users, Properties, Leases, Pending Reviews).

        ii. Monitor Registration Pipeline progress through status filter tabs (Pending → Reviewing → Approved).

        iii. Manage user accounts using role-specific filter pills (All, Tenant, Landlord, Admin) with combined search and filter.

        iv. Access Registration Review dashboard with detailed review modals for applicant evaluation.

        v. Review applicant-uploaded documents and profile photos during application processing.

        vi. Add internal administrative notes to applications for tracking evaluation decisions.

        vii. Update application status with icon-coded badges (Approved, Rejected, Reviewing).

        viii. Access Chat Moderation dashboard for reviewing reported conversations and managing flagged content.

        ix. Access the Consultation Tool for administrative oversight and platform governance.

        x. Provision and set up landlord environments during initial deployment phase.

        xi. Sign out securely from the administrator sidebar.

    b. Enable Landlords to manage their own property deployments independently:

        i. View Dashboard performance via AI-driven KPI Insights with toggle between Simplified Mode and Detailed Analytics.

        ii. Monitor Primary KPIs including Earnings, Active Tenants, Occupancy Rate, and Pending Issues.

        iii. Monitor Extended KPIs including Maintenance Cost, Lease Renewals, and Portfolio Value.

        iv. Analyze Featured Property data including Total Views, Month-over-Month Growth, and Occupancy Rate.

        v. Select preset report ranges (7D, 30D, 90D, 1Y) or define custom date ranges for performance filters.

        vi. View sync status and database connectivity indicators.

        vii. Generate iReside Branded PDF Reports and download CSV Reports for portfolio performance and data auditing.

        viii. Review chronological history of exported reports and audit logs.

        ix. Access the Modular Floor Planner with drag-and-drop structural engine and 20px grid-snapping for designing property layouts.

        x. Interactively manipulate and position corridors, units, and room spaces with real-time save to Supabase backend.

        xi. Specify advance rent requirements and security deposit amounts for units.

        xii. Manage unit media with dedicated slots for Bedroom, Bathroom, Kitchen, and Living areas.

        xiii. Define custom room descriptions, amenities, and features for each unit.

        xiv. Access centralized Maintenance Dashboard for tracking all repair tickets with priority status (Pending, In-Progress, Completed).

        xv. View multi-image galleries and descriptions in maintenance tickets.

        xvi. Monitor active lease progress via live dashboard tracking charts.

        xvii. Access the Smart Document Vault for storing master agreements, reports, and amendments.

        xviii. Calculate and view renewal eligibility windows dynamically.

        xix. Contact assigned tenants via direct messaging and one-click calling.

        xx. Monitor the Live Financial Ledger for overdue and pending invoices with dynamic payment breakdowns.

        xxi. View payment breakdowns including Base Rent, Water, and Electricity components.

        xxii. Create and track invoices for tenant billing.

        xxiii. Record walk-in applications for prospective tenants with required documents and checklist tracking.

        xxiv. Save walk-in applications as pending or approved and update their status.

        xxv. Finalize approved applications into lease records and provision tenant accounts with credentials.

        xxvi. Select lease signing mode (in-person dual signing or remote asynchronous signing) with tenant-first signing order enforcement.

        xxvii. Generate secure JWT-based signing links with 30-day expiration for remote tenant signing.

        xxviii. Access comprehensive audit trail for all signing events with timestamps, IP addresses, and user agents.

        xxix. Navigate to external management tools (Contract Templates, Property Policies, Amenities) with wizard state preservation.

        xxx. Manage Community Hub including approval queues and moderation dashboards.

        xxxi. Publish Management Notices and Utility Alerts for building-wide communication.

        xxxii. Filter and manage community content by specific property.

        xxxiii. Manage amenity bookings for property facilities.

        xxxiv. Record and track utility readings (water, electricity) for billing calculations.

        xxxv. Process move-out requests with inspection checklists and approval workflows.

        xxxvi. Manage lease renewals with customizable renewal settings per property.

        xxxvii. Access inquiry management for prospective tenant questions.

        xxxviii. View recent notifications and activity logs.

    c. Enable Tenants to manage their residency:

        i. View lease details, terms, status, and progress from the tenant dashboard.

        ii. Access the read-only Unit Map for visual representation of the building layout.

        iii. Request unit transfers to vacant units directly from the unit map.

        iv. Submit move-out requests digitally with inspection checklist completion.

        v. Sign digital leases through signature-ready agreements.

        vi. Access the Document Vault for signed reports, lease amendments, and related documents.

        vii. Submit Maintenance Requests with image uploads and detailed descriptions.

        viii. View real-time status updates of repair tickets (Pending, In-Progress, Completed).

        ix. Access the iRis AI Assistant for context-aware building support using RAG-powered natural language queries.

        x. Ask natural language questions about amenities, rules, and lease details via RAG retrieval from tenant profile, property details, unit information, lease details, maintenance requests, and payment history.

        xi. View the Live Financial Ledger for monthly dues and outstanding balances.

        xii. Access chronological payment history and transaction logs.

        xiii. Use the Messaging Portal with typing indicators, read receipts, and presence indicators.

        xiv. Send and receive files or media within conversation threads.

        xv. Access the Community Hub to create Discussion Posts, Photo Albums, and participate in Resident Polls.

        xvi. React to community posts with multi-reactions (Like, Heart, Thumbs Up, Clap, Celebration).

        xvii. Engage in threaded comments on community posts.

        xviii. Bookmark and save important community discussions.

        xix. Report community content for spam or harassment.

        xx. Complete interactive Product Tours for Dashboard, Messages, Lease, and Community modules.

        xxi. Follow step-by-step onboarding flows as a new tenant.

        xxii. Book amenities provided by the landlord/property.

        xxiii. View and manage lease renewals.

    d. Enable both Landlords and Tenants to use the Community Hub (shared module with role-based capabilities):

        i. Create and view Discussion Posts for text-based community threads.

        ii. Create and view Photo Albums for visual updates (up to 4 photos).

        iii. Create and participate in Resident Polls for building-wide decisions.

        iv. View Management Notices pinned for building-wide announcements.

        v. View Utility Alerts for water, power, and maintenance outage notifications.

        vi. React to posts with multi-reactions (Like, Heart, Thumbs Up, Clap, Celebration).

        vii. Engage in threaded comments on community posts in real-time.

        viii. Bookmark and save important community discussions for quick access.

        ix. Report community content for spam or harassment.

    e. For all users, the system shall:

        i. Ensure secure login/logout via Supabase Auth with JWT-based session management.

        ii. Enforce database-level Row Level Security (RLS) for strict data isolation.

        iii. Maintain property-level data isolation where each property deployment operates as a private, independent ecosystem.

        iv. Execute real-time message moderation and toxic content filtering via Groq Llama 3.1 AI.

        v. Maintain smooth UI transitions via Framer Motion animations.

        vi. Provide skeleton loading states for data-heavy components.

        vii. Use modern typography (Geist, Rethink Sans) for enhanced readability.

        viii. Deliver mobile-responsive interfaces across all modules.

        ix. Provide full-page chat interface and floating chat widget for the iRis AI Assistant.

        x. Support conversation history for AI assistant interactions.

2\. To develop iReside using appropriate modern tools and programming technologies to ensure system efficiency and usability:

    a. Framework: Program a web-based application using Next.js 16 (App Router) and TypeScript as the development environment.

    b. Frontend: Implement the UI using React 19, Tailwind CSS 4, Radix UI, and Framer Motion for modern interactivity.

    c. Backend: Utilize Supabase for backend operations, PostgreSQL database management, Real-time functionality, and Storage.

    d. Intelligence: Integrate Groq Llama 3.1 8B via OpenAI SDK for RAG-powered intelligence, AI chat moderation, and landlord analytics insights.

    e. Interactivity: Implement drag-and-drop structural logic using @dnd-kit/core and @dnd-kit/utilities for the Modular Floor Planner.

    f. Security: Enforce authentication and data protection using Supabase Auth and Row-Level Security (RLS) policies.

3\. To determine the level of conformity of iReside to the international standard ISO 25010:

    a. Functional Suitability (Completeness, Correctness, Appropriateness)

    b. Performance Efficiency (Time behavior, Resource utilization, Capacity)

    c. Compatibility (Co-existence, Interoperability)

    d. Interaction Capability (Appropriateness recognizability, Learnability, Operability, User engagement, Inclusivity, User Assistance, Self-descriptiveness)

    e. Reliability (Faultlessness, Availability, Fault tolerance, Recoverability)

    f. Security (Confidentiality, Integrity, Non-repudiation, Accountability, Authenticity)

    g. Maintainability (Modularity, Reusability, Analyzability, Modifiability, Testability)

    h. Flexibility (Adaptability, Scalability, Installability)

    i. Safety (Operational constraints, Risk identification, Fail-safe measures, Hazard warnings)

## **1.3 Purpose and Description** {#1.3-purpose-and-description}

The purpose of this project is to design and develop an integrated, AI-assisted rental management system that enhances operational efficiency, decision-making, and transparency for landlords, property managers, and tenants. The system aims to centralize property operations, automate administrative tasks, and provide intelligent insights through modern web and mobile technologies. It is specifically tailored to address the rental management needs of high-density areas in Valenzuela City.

**iReside: System Feature Specification**

This document provides a comprehensive overview of the current features and technical capabilities implemented within the iReside platform, reflecting the full scope of the integrated property and tenant services ecosystem.

I. AI & Intelligence Engine

1\. iRis AI Assistant (RAG-Powered)

* Contextual Intelligence: Leverages Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to provide tenants with accurate answers about building rules, property amenities, lease details, and local recommendations.  
* Tenant Data Awareness: Integrates directly with tenant profiles, active leases, unit information, recent maintenance requests, and recent payments via the Groq Llama 3.1 8B API for personalized assistance.  
* Natural Language Processing: Allows tenants to ask questions in plain English or Tagalog and receive immediate, context-aware responses.  
* Multi-Access Interface: Available as both a full-page chat interface and a floating chat widget with conversation history support.  
* Fallback Behavior: Returns a graceful fallback response when AI services are unavailable.

2\. AI-Driven Landlord Analytics

* KPI Insights: Analyzes financial and operational metrics (Net Income, Occupancy, Maintenance Volume, Lease Renewals, Portfolio Value) using Groq Llama 3.1 8B.  
* Strategic Recommendations: Provides landlords with concrete next actions based on metric trends and historical performance.  
* Simplified Mode Toggle: Landlords can switch between "Simplified Mode" and "Detailed Analytics" for dashboard reading.  
* Intelligent Fallbacks: Seamlessly transitions to rule-based insights if AI processing is unavailable, ensuring continuous intelligence.

3\. Real-Time Message Moderation

* Toxic Content Filtering: Instant real-time filtering of messages using Groq's Llama 3.1 API to block toxic content, hate speech, and spam before delivery.  
* Safety Enforcement: Ensures a safe communication environment across Landlord–Tenant messaging and Community Hub channels.

II. Property & Asset Management

4\. Unit Map Management (unit map)

* Drag-and-Drop Builder: Structural engine for designing property layouts with interactive manipulation of corridors, units, and room spaces.  
* Grid Snapping: Precise 20px grid alignment system for accurate floor plan creation.  
* Real-Time Persistence: All structural changes are saved in real time to the Supabase backend.  
* Read-Only Tenant View: Tenants can view the building layout as a read-only unit map within their portal.

5\. Property & Unit Configuration

* Property Records Management: Landlords create and manage property records including attributes, amenities, and availability.  
* Unit Definition & Pricing: Landlords define units within a property with pricing, advance rent requirements, security deposits, and customized monthly rates.  
* Media Management: Support for uploading high-quality photos and custom descriptions per unit.  
* Amenity Tagging: Granular tagging of specific unit features for internal management and tenant reference.

III. Applications & Onboarding

6\. Walk-in Application Processing

* Landlord-Initiated Applications: Landlords record walk-in applications for prospective tenants by opening a unit showcase and capturing applicant details.  
* Document & Checklist Management: Storage of applicant details, supporting documents, and checklist progress for each walk-in application.  
* Status Workflow: Landlords can save applications as pending or approved and update their status as the process progresses.  
* Lease Finalization: Approved applications are finalized into lease records with automatic provisioning of a tenant account and credentials.

7\. Tenant Application Tracking

* Live Status Monitoring: Real-time integration with the Supabase backend to track application progress (Reviewing, Approved, etc.).  
* Dynamic Progress Indicators: Interactive UI components that translate database states into user-friendly progress steps.  
* Application Timeline: A chronological view of events, including submission dates, status changes, and required actions.

IV. Lease & Document Management

8\. Digital Lease Management

* Live Lease Progress Tracking: Dynamic visualization of active lease progress, start/end dates, and remaining days.  
* Lease Overview: Both landlords and tenants can view lease details, terms, status, and renewal eligibility.  
* Renewal Eligibility Engine: Dynamically calculates and displays renewal windows based on database records.  
* Smart Document Vault: Centralized access to signed master agreements, verified reports, amendments, and related lease files for both parties.

9\. Enhanced Lease Signing Workflow

* Dual Signing Modes: Supports both in-person (dual) and remote (async) signing modes with tenant-first signing order enforcement.  
* Secure Remote Signing: Generates JWT-based signing links with 30-day expiration for remote tenant signing.  
* Lease Status State Machine: Implements validated lease status transitions (draft → pending\_tenant\_signature → pending\_landlord\_signature → active).  
* Comprehensive Audit Trail: Logs all signing events with timestamps, IP addresses, and user agents for full auditability.  
* Wizard State Persistence: Preserves wizard state in localStorage when navigating to external management tools (Contract Templates, Property Policies, Amenities).  
* Signature Validation: Validates signature data format, size, and dimensions before storage and prevents concurrent signing conflicts.

V. Operations, Reports & Financials

10\. Portfolio Reporting & Auditing

* Branded PDF Reports: Generate professional, iReside-branded PDF performance reports for property portfolios.  
* CSV Data Exports: Downloadable CSV reports for detailed financial audits and external record-keeping.  
* Export History & Audit Logs: A chronological repository of all generated reports and historical performance snapshots.  
* Custom Date Ranges: Preset filters (7D, 30D, 90D, 1Y) and custom date pickers for tailored reporting windows.

11\. Landlord Maintenance Operations

* Centralized Ticket Dashboard: Real-time tracking of maintenance requests with priority-based status (Pending, In-Progress, Completed).  
* Rich Media Documentation: Support for multi-image galleries and detailed tenant descriptions in maintenance tickets to provide context for repairs.  
* Tenant Submission: Tenants can submit maintenance requests with image uploads and detailed descriptions for their unit or property.  
* Status Tracking: Full maintenance request history and real-time status tracking visible to both landlords and tenants.

12\. Real-Time Financial Operations

* Live Financial Ledger: Real-time sync of overdue, pending, and completed rent/utility invoices from the Supabase payments database.  
* Dynamic Invoice Breakdown: Automatic categorization of base rent, water, and electricity with dynamically fetched values.  
* Invoice Management: Landlords can create and track invoices for their tenants.  
* Payment History & Logs: Chronological log of transaction status, payment methods, and timestamps within both the Landlord and Tenant Portals.

VI. Communication & Community

13\. Real-Time Messaging Engine

* Instant Delivery: Powered by Supabase Realtime for lag-free messaging between landlords and tenants.  
* Presence Indicators: Real-time typing indicators, read receipts, and status tracking for improved user engagement.  
* Media & File Sharing: Direct upload of documents and photos within conversation threads.  
* Contacts Sidebar: Conversation management with a contacts list for quick access.

14\. Community Hub

* Discussion Posts: Text-based community threads for building-wide engagement.  
* Photo Albums: Image galleries (up to 4 photos) for visual updates and announcements.  
* Resident Polls: Interactive community polls for gathering resident feedback.  
* Management Notices: Pinned announcements for building-wide updates from landlords.  
* Utility Alerts: Auto-styled notifications for water, power, and maintenance outages.  
* Threaded Comments: Real-time discussion threads on any community post.  
* Post Saving & Reporting: Bookmark important discussions and report spam or harassment.  
* Approval Queue (Landlord): Review and approve/reject resident posts before they go live.  
* Moderation Dashboard (Landlord): Centralized tab for handling pending resident content with property filtering.

VII. Tenant Experience & Onboarding

15\. Tenant Residency Tools

* Unit Map (Read-Only): Visual representation of building layout for tenant orientation.  
* Unit Transfer Requests: Tenants can request transfer to vacant units directly from the unit map.  
* Move-Out Requests: Digital submission of move-out requests through the tenant portal.

16\. Product Tours & Onboarding

* Interactive Guided Tours: Step-by-step product tours for Dashboard, Messages, Lease, and Community modules.  
* Onboarding Flow: Structured introduction for new tenants to familiarize them with the platform's capabilities.

VIII. Security & Infrastructure

17\. Security & Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)

* Database Isolation (RLS): Strict data isolation between Admin, Landlord, and Tenant roles enforced at the PostgreSQL level via Row-Level Security.  
* Secure Authentication: Supabase Auth-powered session persistence with role-aware navigation guards.  
* Destructive Sign-Out: Secure logout functionality with audit-safe session termination.  
* Sensitive Data Protection: System-wide enforcement of data access restrictions based on authenticated role.

18\. Cross-Cutting Platform Concerns

* UI Transitions: Framer Motion animations for polished user experience.  
* Loading States: Skeleton loaders for data-heavy views to maintain responsiveness.  
* Typography: Geist \+ Rethink Sans fonts for consistent visual identity.  
* Responsive Design: Mobile-responsive layouts across all modules and portals.

IX. Admin Portal (Administrator)

19\. System Monitoring Dashboard

* Live Enterprise Metrics: Real-time monitoring of Total Users, Active Properties, Active Leases, and Pending Reviews.  
* Registration Pipeline Visualization: Visual progress tracking for landlord applications (Pending → Reviewing → Approved).  
* Role Breakdown Analytics: Comparative visualization of user roles across the platform.

20\. Registration & User Governance

* Advanced Applicant Review: Detailed Review Modals for evaluating applicant-uploaded documents and profile photos.  
* Internal Administrative Notes: Capability for admins to leave internal notes and evaluation remarks on registration cases.  
* User Management & Moderation: Role-specific filter pills (All, Tenant, Landlord, Admin) and combined name/email search for governance.  
* Status Filter Tabs: Icon-coded status badges for streamlined application processing.

### 	

### **1.3.1 Significance of the Study** {#1.3.1-significance-of-the-study}

	The development of this modular visual environment is significant because it presents a customized technical solution to the specific issues of the rental housing sector in selected high-density rental areas in Valenzuela City. This study's outcomes will help the following stakeholders:

	**Landlords and Property Owners:** The study offers a game-changing approach to moving from unorganized manual record-keeping to a high-visibility "Visual Command Center." By utilizing the Unit Map and color-coded overlays, owners can significantly minimize cognitive burden and operational errors. This helps them to see delayed payments or essential maintenance issues at a glance, thereby safeguarding their property investment and guaranteeing steady cash flow.

	**Property Managers:** The platform is a significant productivity tool that helps to streamline daily activities. The combination of a supported AI utility for maintenance triage and a centralized Administrative Vault lowers administrative workload and enables data-driven decision making. Managers may better track tenant occupancy and prioritize maintenance needs, resulting in greater responsibility and organizational efficiency.

	**Tenants:** This project is essential for the students, workers, and families that live in high residential areas of Valenzuela City and value transparency and modern convenience. The "Seamless Living App" gives them real-time access to their financial responsibilities (Tenant Ledger) and a direct, accountable channel for maintenance reporting. Access to a Digital Handbook also guarantees that they are aware of house regulations, resulting in a stronger connection with management.

	**Local Community and Barangay Officials:** The project encourages rental enterprises to use the Administrative Vault for legal permits and safety certificates, thereby formalizing their operations. This helps to guarantee that dormitories and apartments in the area follow legal guidelines, resulting in a safer, more transparent, and trustworthy housing environment within the barangay.

	**Future Researchers:** This study adds to the field of software development and "Spatial Property Mapping" applications in small- to medium-sized property management. It provides a plan for integrating spatial visualization and AI-powered triage into low-cost, user-friendly software for urban residential communities, as well as a model for future advancements in localized PropTech.

## **1.4 Scope and Delimitation** {#1.4-scope-and-delimitation}

### **Scope**

This project focuses on the design and development of iReside, an exclusive, private property and tenant management ecosystem optimized for the multi-unit rental market within Valenzuela City. iReside operates as a landlord-centric management platform where each property functions as an isolated, logically separated ecosystem, and tenants are provisioned exclusively through approved onboarding workflows. There is no public listing, unit searching, or marketplace discovery functionality.

The system comprises three primary interfaces: a Landlord Portal for comprehensive property and tenant management, a Tenant Portal for residency tools and communication, and a System Administration Portal used by the development team for deployment provisioning, platform health monitoring, and technical maintenance. The platform is delivered through a unified web-based architecture built with Next.js 16 and React 19, featuring both a desktop-optimized dashboard for comprehensive management tasks and a mobile-optimized Progressive Web App (PWA) interface for on-the-go operations. This responsive, cross-platform approach ensures that users can access the system across diverse devices through any modern web browser, with mobile-specific UI patterns and touch-optimized interactions while maintaining feature parity across form factors. System security is enforced through a multi-layered approach using Supabase Auth and PostgreSQL Row Level Security (RLS), maintaining strict data isolation between three defined roles: Admin, Landlord, and Tenant.

**Deployment and Operational Model**

iReside follows a deployment-oriented operational model in which the development team acts solely as the system provider and technical administrator responsible for initial deployment, account provisioning, infrastructure maintenance, and security-related updates. After deployment and onboarding, landlords independently manage their own property ecosystem, including tenant onboarding, lease management, financial records, moderation, and operational workflows.

The platform is designed as a semi-managed system rather than a continuously administered centralized service. Feature enhancements, customization requests, and major version upgrades are treated as optional post-deployment service engagements and are outside the guaranteed scope of the initial system delivery. This operational structure aligns with the study's objective of providing landlords with a self-managed digital property management environment without requiring dependence on a permanent third-party governing institution.

**2D Spatial Canvas & Unit Map**

The core of the system is a 2D Spatial Canvas featuring a unit map, where landlords can digitally map building layouts using a drag-and-drop structural engine with grid-snapping for precise unit alignment. This is enhanced by Dynamic Status Overlays that provide real-time visual alerts for unit occupancy, overdue payments, and active maintenance requests. Tenants can view this floor plan as a read-only unit map and submit unit transfer requests directly from the visual interface for vacant units. The system manages individual unit configurations via a multi-step wizard, allowing for granular pricing, deposit rules, and amenity tagging. The 2D canvas is strictly an organizational visualization tool and does not produce engineering-grade measurements or structural assessments.

**Walk-in Applications & Tenant Onboarding**

Since the system operates as a private, invitation-only platform, tenant onboarding is exclusively managed by landlords through a walk-in application workflow. Landlords open a unit showcase, record applicant details and supporting documents, and manage the application through a status pipeline (Pending → Reviewing → Approved). Upon approval, the system finalizes the application into a lease record and provisions a tenant account with credentials. There is no self-service tenant registration or public application portal \- all tenant accounts originate from landlord-initiated or admin-initiated onboarding processes.

**Financial Management**

iReside implements a comprehensive Real-Time Financial Ledger that synchronizes overdue, pending, and completed rent and utility invoices. The system supports Dynamic Invoice Breakdowns covering base rent, water, and electricity charges, and landlords can create and track invoices for their tenants. Landlords can also generate professional PDF reports and CSV data exports for auditing and historical performance snapshots. The financial ledger is designed for operational tracking only and is not a substitute for formal accounting systems; it does not execute actual fund transfers, integrate with payment gateways or banking APIs, or compute tax obligations and government remittances.

**Analytics and Performance Metrics**

The system features a centralized dashboard that tracks eight core Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) to provide landlords with data-driven operational oversight. These include Financial Metrics such as Total Rent Collected, Unpaid Rent, and Net Operating Income (NOI), which serves as the definitive measure of a property's profitability by subtracting all operating expenses from total revenue; Occupancy Metrics, which differentiate between physical Occupied Units and Revenue Efficiency, a critical standard for measuring realized income against the property's total revenue potential; and Operational Metrics, specifically Tenant Churn to monitor turnover patterns and Maintenance Speed, which calculates the average resolution time for repair tickets to assess service quality. All metrics are automatically generated from internal system transactions and maintenance logs, with the ability to toggle between high-level summaries and detailed breakdown views. The analytics module is limited to property-level performance and does not function as full-scale accounting software; it does not handle external tax filings, payroll management, or complex asset depreciation. While the system identifies financial trends, it does not provide legal or financial advice regarding investment decisions.

**AI-Powered Features**

The system integrates supportive AI utilities powered by Groq Llama 3.1 8B via Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). These include the iRis AI Assistant, which provides tenants with contextual intelligence regarding building rules, property amenities, lease details, and recent payments; AI-Driven Landlord Analytics, which analyzes operational metrics such as net income, occupancy, and maintenance activity to surface strategic recommendations with a toggle between simplified and detailed modes; Real-Time Message Moderation, which filters toxic content, hate speech, and spam within communication channels; and Maintenance Triage, which uses natural language processing to categorize the severity of maintenance reports. All AI outputs are strictly advisory and require human confirmation before any action is taken. The AI does not autonomously approve budgets, contracts, or legal decisions, and does not perform credit scoring or automated tenant background checks.

**Communication & Community**

A Real-Time Messaging Engine powered by Supabase Realtime facilitates instant communication between landlords and tenants, with presence indicators, typing indicators, read receipts, and media/file sharing capabilities within conversation threads. In addition, a Community Hub provides a centralized space for building-wide engagement, supporting discussion posts, photo albums, resident polls, management notices, utility alerts, threaded comments, post saving, and content reporting. Landlords manage community content through an approval queue and moderation dashboard. Communication is limited to in-app text exchanges and media sharing. Communication records are retained for operational reference, though they are not designed for legal discovery purposes.

**System Administration Portal**

A limited-access System Administration Portal is used by the development team for deployment provisioning, landlord account verification, platform health monitoring, and technical maintenance activities. The portal provides Live Metrics covering total users, active properties, active leases, and the registration pipeline, and includes tools for Advanced Applicant Review, enabling verification of landlord registrations through uploaded business permits and government IDs via detailed review modals. Administrators do not manage day-to-day landlord operations, intervene in landlord-tenant transactions, or govern individual property ecosystems \- these responsibilities rest entirely with landlords after their accounts are provisioned. Role-specific filtering and internal administrative notes on registration cases are available for technical governance purposes only.

 

**Delimitations**

The study is subject to the following constraints and exclusions, each of which reflects deliberate boundaries set to maintain research focus, operational feasibility, and legal compliance.

 

**A. Geographic and Demographic Boundaries**

The system is limited to residential multi-unit properties \- apartments, dormitories, and boarding houses \- within Barangay Marulas, Canumay, Malinta, and Maysan in Valenzuela City. Single-family homes, transient hotels, and commercial properties are excluded, as these require distinct regulatory frameworks and management workflows not applicable to multi-unit residential tenancy. Expansion beyond these four barangays is not attempted, as doing so would require coordination with multiple barangay councils, dilute the research focus, and prevent the team from establishing the local trust and physical verification capacity necessary for meaningful user testing. Tenant demographics are not segmented by age, income, or employment type, in observance of Data Privacy Act requirements and to avoid enabling discriminatory filtering within the platform.

**B. Property and Unit Modeling Constraints**

The Unit Map is an organizational visualization tool only and does not produce engineering-grade measurements, structural load computations, building code compliance assessments, or fire safety evaluations. These activities constitute professional practice restricted to licensed engineers and architects under Philippine law. The unit map uses a simplified rectangular grid and does not support irregular or curved geometry, as such features would introduce significant development complexity while offering minimal added value given that the vast majority of multi-unit residential properties in the target barangays use standard rectangular unit layouts. Three-dimensional rendering, photorealistic visualization, and BIM integration are likewise excluded. Unit status overlays are informational and do not automatically trigger maintenance dispatch or penalty enforcement, as automated actions of this nature require verified contractor networks and a liability framework the platform has not established. The system does not generate automated eviction notices, as eviction is a judicial process requiring attorney supervision under Batas Pambansa Blg. 25\.

**C. Access Model and Tenant Onboarding Constraints**

The platform operates exclusively as a private, landlord-managed system. There is no public-facing discovery portal, unit listing, property search, map-based filtering, or self-service tenant registration. All tenant accounts are provisioned by landlords or the development team through approved onboarding and lease-finalization workflows. This design is intentional \- tenant access to any property is gated by the landlord's explicit approval process, reflecting the real-world landlord-tenant onboarding dynamic in the target market.

**D. Messaging and Communication Limits**

Messaging is limited to in-app text and does not include voice calls or video conferencing, which would require WebRTC infrastructure and significant bandwidth resources inconsistent with the target demographic's connectivity. The system uses standard HTTPS/TLS transport security and database encryption at rest but does not implement end-to-end encryption, as doing so would prevent moderation and create key recovery complications for legally significant communications such as lease discussions and payment confirmations. The platform does not integrate with external SMS gateways or email marketing systems, nor does it support user-programmable notification workflows. Automated notification rules are limited to predefined events such as payment reminders, maintenance updates, and lease expiration alerts, which cover the majority of operational communication needs.

**E. Lease and Document Handling**

The digital lease signing workflow supports canvas-based signatures but does not provide third-party notarization, as remote digital notarization is not legally recognized in the Philippines and requires physical appearance before a commissioned notary public under the 2004 Rules on Notarial Practice. The platform does not assess the legal enforceability of uploaded contracts, provide dynamically generated legal clause templates, manage dispute resolution or court filing processes, or perform automated redlining and version comparison of lease amendments. These activities either constitute the practice of law under Rule 138 of the Rules of Court or require ongoing monitoring of legislative changes that are beyond the scope of this study. Document templates are provided in basic form with clearly marked customization sections, with the expectation that landlords consult legal counsel for contract-specific provisions.

**F. Financial Operations and Payments**

The system records payment schedules and invoice history but does not execute actual fund transfers or integrate with payment gateways, bank APIs, or GCash APIs. Operating as a payment intermediary would require BSP registration under Circular 649, AMLA compliance, KYC infrastructure, and PCI-DSS certification \- obligations that are not feasible within the scope of an academic research project. Receipt verification is performed manually by landlords reviewing uploaded GCash screenshots, and no automated OCR-based fraud detection is included. The ledger does not compute tax obligations, VAT filings, or government remittance schedules, and is not a substitute for formal accounting books maintained under BIR regulations. Currency support is limited to Philippine Peso.

**G. Maintenance and Work Orders**

Maintenance requests are categorized through AI assistance but do not automatically assign contractors, as doing so would require a vetted vendor network and a formal liability framework. The platform does not include vendor bidding, procurement workflows, inventory management, or integration with external facilities management platforms. Work order scheduling is basic and does not optimize staff routing or labor allocation. Building inspection schedules, safety certification renewals, and compliance tracking remain the landlord's responsibility and are not managed within the system.

**H. AI Utility Boundaries**

All AI-generated outputs within iReside are advisory and require human confirmation before any action is taken. The AI does not autonomously approve budgets, contracts, or legal decisions, and does not provide legal advice or guarantee the accuracy of its recommendations, as doing so would constitute unauthorized practice of law under Rule 138 of the Rules of Court. The AI knowledge base is configured by landlords from curated content and does not perform real-time web scraping, which would introduce unreliable sources, copyright complications, and unpredictable outputs. Credit scoring and automated tenant background checks are excluded due to the unavailability of credit bureau partnerships, the Data Privacy Act's requirements for human review of automated decisions affecting individuals, and the risk of algorithmic bias against informal workers and unbanked populations.

**I. Administrative Governance**

The System Administration Portal is used exclusively by the development team during the initial deployment and landlord provisioning phase. Document presence is validated and uploaded permits and IDs are visually inspected as part of the landlord onboarding workflow; however, the system does not authenticate documents against government databases, as the relevant agencies do not provide public-facing APIs and formal data-sharing agreements require institutional accreditation not available to this research project. The portal does not include full compliance auditing, periodic regulatory reporting, or management of landlord licensing renewals and tax registration. Administrative access is strictly limited to platform provisioning, technical oversight, and security maintenance \- it does not extend to managing day-to-day landlord operations, intervening in landlord-tenant transactions, or arbitrating legal disputes, which must be resolved through barangay conciliation or the appropriate courts under existing Philippine law.

**J. Security and Privacy Constraints**

The system employs role-based access control with strong authentication but does not implement zero-trust networking or enterprise security integrations, as these exceed the requirements and budget of a research-phase platform. Data is hosted on cloud infrastructure; on-premise deployments are not supported. The platform requires an active internet connection and does not support offline-first synchronization, as conflict resolution for offline data modifications would introduce significant architectural complexity. Biometric authentication is excluded due to Data Privacy Act obligations for sensitive personal information, liveness detection requirements, and device compatibility constraints. Government-issued IDs submitted during landlord vetting are retained only as long as necessary for verification purposes, consistent with data minimization principles under RA 10173\.

**K. Device, Platform, and Integration Limits**

The platform is delivered as a unified web application built with Next.js 16 and React 19, providing both desktop-optimized and mobile-optimized Progressive Web App (PWA) interfaces through responsive design patterns. The mobile experience features touch-optimized UI components, screen-based navigation, and mobile-first CSS modules that adapt to smaller viewports while maintaining full feature access. The system supports all modern web browsers on desktop, Android, and iOS devices through standard web technologies. Native mobile applications for Android or iOS are not included in the current version, as developing and maintaining separate native codebases would exceed the capstone timeline and budget, and the PWA approach provides adequate cross-platform coverage for the target user base. The system does not provide open APIs for third-party developers, integrate with government housing or national ID verification databases, or connect with external listing marketplaces. Data export is limited to operational PDF reports and CSV files; large-scale data warehousing and BI platform integrations are outside the scope of this study. Push notifications may be delivered through browser-based web push APIs where supported by the user's device and browser.

**L. Analytics, Reporting, and Content**

Reporting within the system is operational and descriptive. Predictive analytics, forecasting, and dynamic pricing optimization are excluded, as these require multi-year historical datasets, external economic data integrations, and machine learning expertise beyond the research team's capacity. The system does not include a custom BI dashboard builder or blend external data sources into its analytics. Listing photo authenticity is verified only through basic format validation and duplicate detection; reverse image search and computer vision fraud detection are not implemented. The platform does not support 360-degree virtual tours, AR features, or 3D rendering. Media is compressed and stored for web-optimized performance rather than archival quality. The system does not provide multilingual localization beyond English and basic Filipino terminology, as this language combination is considered sufficient for the target users in the four covered barangays.

**M. Community Hub Constraints**

The Community Hub supports building-wide engagement through posts, polls, photo albums, and announcements within the private property ecosystem. Posts are limited to pre-defined types (Discussion, Photo Album, Resident Poll, Management Notice, Utility Alert), and the platform does not support custom post formats, threaded sub-communities, or public-facing social media integration. Content moderation is handled through landlord approval queues and user-driven content reporting, but the platform does not implement automated AI-based moderation for community posts beyond the messaging layer.

**N. Legal, Ethical, and Operational Boundaries**

The platform does not provide legal counsel on tenancy disputes or eviction procedures, generate government compliance reports, or make automated housing denial decisions. Any automated decision-making that could affect tenant rights must involve meaningful human review in compliance with Section 20 of the Data Privacy Act (RA 10173). The system does not collect biometric, medical, or financial credit data, consistent with data minimization principles and fair housing considerations. Terms of use and privacy policies included in this research version are generic and should be reviewed by legal counsel prior to any commercial deployment. The platform assumes that landlord-provided data is accurate and does not perform independent third-party verification of property details. Standard cloud hosting availability applies; the system does not guarantee high availability SLAs, and disaster recovery is limited to regular database backups. The current version does not support automated lease renewal or IoT integrations, and does not include advanced GIS layers, cadastral mapping, multi-city scaling, or native iOS/Android applications \- all of which are identified as future work contingent on regulatory partnership, infrastructure investment, and post-research commercialization.

**O. Deployment Ownership and Maintenance Boundaries**

The study does not establish a centralized institutional operator such as a barangay office, homeowners association, or government agency. Instead, the platform follows a deployment-based delivery model in which the development team provisions and deploys the system for participating landlords, who thereafter independently manage their own operational environment. Continuous feature development, large-scale operational staffing, and dedicated enterprise support teams are outside the scope of the research.

The development team's responsibilities are limited to initial deployment support, critical security maintenance, and optional post-deployment updates or customization requests subject to separate service arrangements. The study does not evaluate long-term commercial SaaS operations, enterprise-level customer support infrastructure, or nationwide deployment scalability.

## **1.5 Theoretical Framework** {#1.5-theoretical-framework}

![][image1]

#### *Figure 1: Hybrid Framework for Information Systems Success and Situational Awareness* {#figure-1:-hybrid-framework-for-information-systems-success-and-situational-awareness}

The study utilizes a Hybrid Theoretical Framework that synthesizes three established theories: the Task-Technology Fit (TTF) Theory, Endsley’s Situation Awareness (SA) Theory, and the DeLone & McLean (D\&M) Information Systems Success Model. This integration is necessary to address the specific challenge of the study: transitioning rental management from static, text-based records to a dynamic, visual spatial environment.

### **1.5.1 Task-Technology Fit (TTF) Theory** {#1.5.1-task-technology-fit-(ttf)-theory}

Proposed by Goodhue and Thompson (1995), Task-Technology Fit argues that information technology (IT) is more likely to have a positive impact on individual performance and be used if the capabilities of the IT match the tasks that the user must perform.

In the context of **iReside**, the "Task" involves the complex management of multi-unit properties in Barangays Marulas, Maysan, Malinta, and Canumay, specifically monitoring occupancy, tracking payment delinquency, and triaging maintenance requests. The "Technology" refers to the proposed system’s unique features, particularly the **unit map** and the **AI Utility**.

**Relevance:** Traditional spreadsheets (Technology) often fail to provide a quick overview of spatial data (Task), resulting in a low "Fit." **iReside** improves this fit by offering a spatial canvas where the technology (visual blocks) directly aligns with the cognitive task of managing physical space. If the system effectively reduces the landlord's cognitive load, the TTF is high, leading to improved performance.

### **1.5.2 Situation Awareness (SA) Theory** {#1.5.2-situation-awareness-(sa)-theory}

Developed by Mica Endsley (1995), Situation Awareness is defined as "the perception of elements in the environment within a volume of time and space, the comprehension of their meaning, and the projection of their status in the near future." This theory is critical for the design of the **Visual Command Center**.

* **Level 1 (Perception):** The system enables landlords to perceive data immediately through the **Dynamic Status Overlays** (e.g., seeing a unit turn "Red" for overdue or "Orange Pulse" for maintenance).  
* **Level 2 (Comprehension):** The landlord synthesizes this visual data to understand the current state of the property (e.g., understanding that a specific wing of the building has high delinquency).  
* **Level 3 (Projection):** The system utilizes AI-driven analytics to forecast potential future states, such as identifying looming maintenance cycles or predicting occupancy trends. To maintain **AI Utility Boundaries**, these projections are strictly **advisory**. They rely exclusively on landlord-configured data, excluding external web-scraping or automated credit scoring, to provide "human-in-the-loop" decision support. This ensures that while the system anticipates risks, the final "meeting of minds" and fiduciary responsibility remain with the human user..  
* **Relevance:** By moving from static text to dynamic visualization, the system enhances the user's SA, enabling faster and more accurate decision-making compared to traditional manual methods.

  ### **1.5.3 DeLone & McLean (D\&M) IS Success Model** {#1.5.3-delone-&-mclean-(d&m)-is-success-model}

  The updated D\&M model (2003) is used to evaluate the overall success of the system based on six dimensions: Information Quality, System Quality, Service Quality, Intention to Use, User Satisfaction, and Net Benefits.

* **System & Information Quality:** Focuses on the accuracy of the **Tenant Ledger**, the responsiveness of the **Seamless Living App**, and the reliability of the notification system.  
* **Service Quality:** Is represented by the **AI Utility** acting as a support mechanism to assist landlords in triaging issues.  
* **Net Benefits:** The ultimate goal of the study is to provide tangible benefits: reduced administrative workload for landlords and transparent, convenient living conditions for tenants.

  **1.5.4 Synthesis (The Hybrid Model)**

  The researchers contend that **System Quality** (D\&M) creates the foundation. When this system is applied to property management, the **Task-Technology Fit** (TTF) determines how useful the **Unit Map** is. A high degree of fit leads to enhanced **Situation Awareness** (SA) for the landlord. Consequently, high Situation Awareness leads to **User Satisfaction** and **Net Benefits** (Efficiency and Transparency), verifying the success of the **iReside** platform.


  


  

## **1.6 Conceptual Framework** {#1.6-conceptual-framework}

#### *Figure 2: Input Process Output (IPO) Model of the iReside* {#figure-2:-input-process-output-(ipo)-model-of-the-ireside}

This study's conceptual framework is based on the Input-Process-Output (IPO) model, which provides a structured method to analyze the development and operational flows of the iReside platform. This approach demonstrates how combining modern software engineering, stakeholder needs, and iterative development cycles resulted in an exclusive, private property and tenant management ecosystem for the multi-unit rental market within Valenzuela City. iReside is designed as a landlord-centric management platform where each property functions as an isolated, private ecosystem, tenants are provisioned exclusively through approved onboarding workflows, and there is no public listing, unit searching, or marketplace discovery. The system remains flexible by leveraging a continuous feedback loop, ensuring that the end result adequately addresses the practical challenges of local rental management within this private-access architecture.

The Input phase of the framework encompasses the fundamental elements required to initiate the research and development effort. This begins with a comprehensive knowledge base gathered from related literature and existing systems to identify technological gaps in current property management solutions, particularly those serving the multi-unit residential rental market in Barangays Marulas, Canumay, Malinta, and Maysan within Valenzuela City.

Technically, the input comprises a modern full-stack architecture: Next.js 16 (App Router) with TypeScript for the application framework, React 19 with Tailwind CSS 4, Radix UI, and Framer Motion for a polished, responsive frontend, and Supabase for secure PostgreSQL database management, real-time subscriptions, authentication, and cloud storage. Specialized libraries include @dnd-kit/core for the drag-and-drop spatial canvas and Geist \+ Rethink Sans for typographic consistency. The integration of the Groq Llama 3.1 8B API via the OpenAI SDK provides the intelligent layer powering the iRis AI Assistant, landlord analytics, message moderation, and maintenance triage through Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). These software tools are supported by hardware resources such as high-performance laptops for development and smartphones for responsive design testing, with the application hosted on cloud infrastructure through Supabase. Standard TCP/IP protocols are used over Wi-Fi and mobile data to provide seamless real-time synchronization across all user roles.

Additionally, domain-specific inputs inform the system's design: the private-access model (where properties operate as isolated ecosystems analogous to private servers), the three-role permission structure (Admin, Landlord, Tenant), and the walk-in application workflow that gates all tenant provisioning through landlord approval — reflecting the real-world onboarding dynamics of the target market.

The Process phase focuses on the transformation of these inputs through the Evolutionary Prototyping Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC). Unlike linear models, this iterative process begins by identifying core property and rental management needs specific to the residential landscapes of Barangays Canumay, Maysan, Malinta, and Marulas within Valenzuela City, anchored around the platform's private, landlord-controlled ecosystem model.

Following the initial requirement gathering, the process moves into the design of three distinct user interfaces, the Landlord Portal for comprehensive property, tenant, and financial management; the Tenant Portal for residency tools, communication, and AI-assisted support; and the Administrator Portal for platform governance and system monitoring, along with the structuring of database schemas with Row-Level Security (RLS) to enforce strict data isolation between roles.

The development stage involves active coding and the integration of core system modules: the 2D Spatial Canvas with unit map for unit map management, the walk-in application and lease finalization workflows for tenant onboarding, the enhanced digital lease signing system with dual and remote signing modes, the real-time financial ledger for invoice and payment tracking, the maintenance request management pipeline, the real-time messaging engine with AI-powered content moderation, the iRis AI Assistant with RAG capabilities, the Community Hub for building-wide engagement, and the AI-driven landlord analytics dashboard with reporting and export tools.

This is immediately followed by a rigorous user validation phase, where direct feedback is gathered from a diverse group of landlords and tenants across the four identified barangays to identify necessary functional adjustments. This leads to a systematic refinement stage where the platform is updated based on actual user experience, ensuring that the final delivery is technically robust, culturally relevant, and user-centric. This cycle repeats iteratively until the platform fully meets the established project requirements and is deemed ready for deployment across the target locales.

The primary output of this study is iReside: Integrated Property and Tenant Management Platform, a unified web-based application delivered through both desktop-optimized dashboards and a mobile-optimized Progressive Web App (PWA) interface accessible via any modern web browser. The platform comprises three role-specific portals:

Landlord Portal \- featuring the unit map, walk-in application processing, lease management with enhanced digital signing, real-time financial ledger, maintenance dashboard, AI-driven analytics with PDF/CSV reporting, real-time messaging, and community management tools.

Tenant Portal \- providing digital lease signing, a document vault, lease overview with renewal eligibility, a read-only unit map with transfer request capability, maintenance request submission, payment history, real-time messaging with the landlord, the iRis AI Assistant, community engagement, and interactive product tours for onboarding.

Administrator Portal \- offering live enterprise metrics, registration pipeline monitoring, advanced applicant review with document inspection, user governance with role-specific filtering, and internal administrative notes.

Beyond the application itself, the outputs include comprehensive user documentation with role-specific guides for landlords, tenants, and administrators, ensuring high adoption rates. Detailed technical documentation is produced, defining the system architecture, API standards, database schemas, and security policies for future maintenance and scaling. Finally, a feedback mechanism is built into the framework, allowing for continuous analysis of operational data and user input. This ensures that the system can be refined even after deployment, keeping it relevant and efficient in the long-term administration of rental properties within the private ecosystem model.

## **1.7 Definition of Terms** {#1.7-definition-of-terms}

* **AI-Driven Landlord Analytics** – A feature of the AI Utility that analyzes operational and financial metrics, Net Income, Occupancy Rate, Maintenance Volume, Lease Renewals, and Portfolio Value, using Groq-hosted Llama 3.1 8B to surface strategic recommendations for landlords in both simplified and detailed display modes.  
* **AI Maintenance Triage** – A feature of the AI Utility that analyzes text descriptions of unit damages (via the Smart Maintenance Reporting system) to automatically categorize the issue (e.g., Plumbing, Electrical), determine its severity, and suggest appropriate actions or estimated costs.  
* **Artificial Intelligence (AI) Utility / Supportive AI Utility** – A supportive component of the system that uses third-party APIs for natural language processing to assist landlords in operational tasks, such as triaging maintenance reports, categorizing urgency, and providing data-driven analytics on payment and tenant behavior trends.  
* **Centralized Management Hub**  – The landlord-facing dashboard interface that consolidates real-time operational data, the Unit Map, and Dynamic Status Overlays into a single spatial view, enabling landlords to monitor property status, identify issues, and make informed decisions with minimal cognitive load.  
* **Community Hub** – A module within iReside that facilitates property-based engagement, allowing landlords to publish announcements, utility alerts, and pinned notices, while tenants can participate in community discussions, polls, and photo albums, all moderated through a landlord-controlled approval queue.  
* **DeLone & McLean (D\&M) Information Systems Success Model** – The model used in the study to evaluate the overall success of the iReside system across dimensions such as System Quality, Information Quality, Service Quality, User Satisfaction, and Net Benefits.  
* **Digital Lease Signing** – The process within the Lease Management Module that allows landlords and tenants to execute lease agreements electronically through in-person or remote signing workflows, with dual-signature support and validated state transitions tracked through the Lease Status State Machine.  
* **Digital Twin** – The conceptual goal of the platform, which is to create a continuous, real-time digital representation of the physical rental property, specifically linking the status of real-world units (e.g., payment status) to their visual "blocks" on the virtual canvas.  
* **Dynamic Invoice Breakdowns** – A feature of the Payments and Billing Module that itemizes tenant invoices into base rent, water, and electricity charges, enabling granular financial tracking for both landlords and tenants.  
* **Dynamic Status Overlays** – A color-coded visualization system that updates the unit blocks on the Unit Map in real-time to provide instant situational awareness of a unit's status, such as Green (Vacant), Blue (Occupied/Paid), Yellow (Due Soon), Red (Overdue Payment), and Orange Pulse (Active Maintenance).  
* **Groq-hosted Llama 3.1 8B** – The large language model integrated via Groq's inference engine that powers iReside's AI features, including the iRis AI Assistant, Real-Time Message Moderation, and AI-Driven Landlord Analytics, through a RAG-based architecture.  
* **iReside** – The proposed Integrated Rental Management System, which is a dual-platform (Web and Mobile) ecosystem designed to streamline property operations and enhance transparency for landlords and tenants in Barangay Marulas, Valenzuela City.  
* **iRis AI Assistant** – Within the iReside ecosystem, iRis refers to the proprietary AI service powered by Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). It functions as a dual-purpose support tool that provides tenants with immediate, fact-grounded answers regarding their lease terms and building rules, while simultaneously assisting landlords by automatically triaging and prioritizing maintenance requests based on the urgency and nature of the reported issue.  
* **Lease Status State Machine** – The validated workflow within the Lease Management Module that enforces strict lease status transitions: draft → pending\_tenant\_signature → pending\_landlord\_signature → active, ensuring leases proceed through all required approval steps.  
* **Managed-Access Portal** – The tenant-facing component of iReside, in which each tenant's access is restricted to their enrolled property and unit, ensuring a secure and professionally managed digital environment where participation is directly tied to active lease status.  
* **Multi-Tenant Data Isolation** – The architectural principle within iReside by which each property operates as a private, independent ecosystem, with database-level Row-Level Security (RLS) enforcing strict data separation between properties, landlords, and tenants.  
* **Progressive Web App (PWA)** – The mobile delivery mechanism of iReside, enabling the Seamless Living App to function as a mobile-optimized, installable application accessible through any modern web browser while maintaining full responsiveness and offline-like behavior.  
* **Real-Time Message Moderation** – A security feature powered by Groq Llama 3.1 8B that performs instant real-time filtering of toxic content, hate speech, and spam within landlord-tenant messaging channels and the Community Hub, blocking unsafe messages before delivery.  
* **Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)** – An AI architecture that combines retrieved document context with large language model generation to produce accurate, fact-grounded responses. In iReside, RAG powers the iRis AI Assistant by retrieving relevant property management documentation and local ordinances from a pgvector-enabled knowledge base to answer tenant and landlord queries.  
* **Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)** – The security architecture within the iReside platform that differentiates permissions for Landlords, Tenants, and Administrators, ensuring each user accesses only information and actions scoped to their role and enrolled property.  
* **Row-Level Security (RLS)** – A database-level enforcement mechanism implemented through PostgreSQL that enforces strict data isolation between Admin, Landlord, and Tenant roles, ensuring no cross-role or cross-property data access is possible at the infrastructure layer.  
* **Seamless Living App** – The user-friendly mobile interface for tenants, providing them with a personal residency dashboard for transparent payment tracking, access to the Digital Handbook, and a direct channel for maintenance reporting.  
* **Situation Awareness (SA) Theory** – A theoretical framework, critical for the design of the centralized management hub , that defines the user's ability to perceive, comprehend, and project the status of elements in their environment, which the system enhances through its Dynamic Status Overlays and predictive analytics.
