| TC\_Community\_Reporting\_079 | Community Reporting (/tenant/community) | Report a community post with a reason | At least one reportable post exists in the community feed. | 1\. Open /tenant/community. 2\. Choose Report on a post. 3\. Enter a report reason and submit. | The system accepts the report and records the post for moderation review. |   |   |   |
| TC\_Unit\_Map\_080 | Unit Map (/tenant/unit-map) | Open the read-only tenant unit map | A mapped property layout exists for the tenant's property. | 1\. Open /tenant/unit-map. 2\. Wait for the layout to render. 3\. Review the displayed unit map. | The tenant can view the building layout in read-only mode. |   |   |   |
| TC\_Unit\_Transfer\_Requests\_081 | Unit Transfer Requests (/tenant/unit-map) | Submit a unit transfer request for an eligible vacant unit | The tenant has an active lease and an eligible vacant unit is available. | 1\. Open /tenant/unit-map. 2\. Select a vacant unit. 3\. Enter a transfer reason and submit the request. | The system creates the transfer request and marks it pending for landlord review. |   |   |   |
| TC\_Unit\_Transfer\_Requests\_082 | Unit Transfer Requests (/tenant/unit-map) | Prevent duplicate pending unit transfer requests | The tenant already has a pending transfer request for the property. | 1\. Open /tenant/unit-map. 2\. Attempt to submit another transfer request. 3\. Review the response. | The system blocks the duplicate request and informs the tenant that a pending transfer request already exists. |   |   |   |
| TC\_Move\_Out\_Request\_083 | Move-Out Request (/tenant/lease or dashboard component) | Start a move-out request and detect unsettled balances | The tenant can open the move-out request flow. | 1\. Trigger Request Move-Out. 2\. Wait for the clearance scan to complete. 3\. Review the result. | The system scans ledger balances and shows a blocked clearance state when unsettled balances are found. |   |   |   |
| TC\_Iris\_Ai\_Assistant\_084 | iRis AI Assistant | Ask iRis a contextual tenant support question | The tenant is logged in and the iRis assistant is available. | 1\. Open the iRis assistant from the dashboard or messages tray. 2\. Ask a valid question about lease, amenities, rules, or support. 3\. Wait for the reply. | The system returns a contextual assistant response based on the tenant's available system data. |   |   |   |
| TC\_Iris\_Ai\_Assistant\_Fallback\_085 | iRis AI Assistant Fallback | Show a fallback response when the AI service is unavailable | The tenant can access the iRis assistant and the AI request can be forced to fail or time out. | 1\. Open the iRis assistant. 2\. Submit a question while the AI service is unavailable. 3\. Review the returned response. | The system displays a fallback assistance message instead of leaving the tenant without a reply. |   |   |   |
| **Admin** |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| TC\_Authentication\_086 | Authentication (/login) | Log in with valid administrator credentials | An administrator account exists and is active. | 1\. Open /login. 2\. Enter valid administrator credentials. 3\. Click Log In. | The system authenticates the administrator and routes the user to the admin portal. |   |   |   |
| TC\_Authentication\_087 | Authentication (/login) | Reject invalid administrator credentials | The login page is accessible. | 1\. Open /login. 2\. Enter invalid administrator credentials. 3\. Click Log In. | The system denies access and does not open admin-only routes. |   |   |   |
| TC\_Admin\_Dashboard\_088 | Admin Dashboard (/admin/dashboard) | Load platform metrics on the admin dashboard | The administrator is logged in and platform data is accessible. | 1\. Open /admin/dashboard. 2\. Wait for the statistics cards to load. 3\. Review user, property, and lease metrics. | The admin dashboard shows platform-wide metrics and quick actions for governance tasks. |   |   |   |
| TC\_Admin\_Dashboard\_089 | Admin Dashboard (/admin/dashboard) | Show a valid fallback state when admin metrics cannot be retrieved | The admin dashboard is accessible but metrics are unavailable. | 1\. Open /admin/dashboard. 2\. Simulate or observe a failed metrics fetch. 3\. Review the rendered state. | The dashboard remains usable and shows a loading, zero-value, or fallback state instead of crashing. |   |   |   |
| TC\_Admin\_Rbac\_090 | Admin RBAC (/admin/\*) | Restrict admin routes to authorized administrators only | Admin routes exist and non-admin accounts are available for testing. | 1\. Attempt to open an admin route while logged out. 2\. Attempt again as a landlord or tenant. 3\. Attempt again as an admin. | Only the administrator can access admin routes, while others are denied or redirected. |   |   |   |
| TC\_Users\_091 | Users (/admin/users) | Load the platform user list with role counts | User records exist in the platform. | 1\. Open /admin/users. 2\. Wait for the user list and counts to load. 3\. Review the displayed totals and rows. | The page shows registered users and the role-based counts needed for governance review. |   |   |   |
| TC\_Users\_092 | Users (/admin/users) | Search platform users by name or email | Multiple user accounts exist in the platform. | 1\. Open /admin/users. 2\. Enter a name or email in the search input. 3\. Review the filtered list. | Only users matching the name or email query remain in the result set. |   |   |   |
| TC\_Users\_093 | Users (/admin/users) | Filter users by role pill | Users exist across multiple roles. | 1\. Open /admin/users. 2\. Click the Tenant, Landlord, Admin, and All pills. 3\. Review the user rows after each selection. | The user list updates correctly for each role filter and the counts remain consistent with the selected pill. |   |   |   |
| TC\_Users\_094 | Users (/admin/users) | Show an empty state when no user search results match | The admin user page is accessible. | 1\. Open /admin/users. 2\. Enter a search term that matches no user. 3\. Review the page response. | The page shows a no-results state and prompts the admin to adjust filters or search terms. |   |   |   |
| TC\_Landlord\_Registrations\_Review\_095 | Landlord Registrations Review | Load pending landlord registrations for review | Pending landlord registration submissions exist in the system. | 1\. Open the landlord registration review module. 2\. Wait for the registration list to load. 3\. Review the pending submissions. | The system displays submitted landlord registrations that are available for administrator review. |   |   |   |
| TC\_Landlord\_Registrations\_Review\_096 | Landlord Registrations Review | Open a landlord registration and inspect its submitted details | At least one landlord registration submission exists. | 1\. Open the landlord registration review list. 2\. Select a submission. 3\. Review the form data, notes, and supporting files. | The administrator can inspect the selected registration details and supporting documents for decision-making. |   |   |   |
| TC\_Landlord\_Registrations\_Review\_097 | Landlord Registrations Review | Approve a landlord registration with internal notes | A pending landlord registration is available for approval. | 1\. Open a pending registration. 2\. Enter an internal note if required. 3\. Approve the submission. | The registration status updates to approved and the decision is saved in the review history. |   |   |   |
| TC\_Landlord\_Registrations\_Review\_098 | Landlord Registrations Review | Reject or return a landlord registration for clarification | A pending registration is available for review. | 1\. Open a landlord registration. 2\. Enter rejection or clarification notes. 3\. Reject or return the submission. | The system records the decision and updates the registration to the correct non-approved status. |   |   |   |
| TC\_Governance\_User\_Management\_099 | Governance / User Management | Prevent invalid governance or user management updates | A governance or user update action is available to the administrator. | 1\. Open a governance or user management action. 2\. Enter invalid or unauthorized update data. 3\. Attempt to save the change. | The system blocks the invalid update and preserves current records until valid data is submitted. |   |   |   |
| TC\_Admin\_Sign\_Out\_100 | Admin Sign-out | Sign out securely from the admin portal | The administrator is currently logged in. | 1\. Open the admin portal. 2\. Use the sign-out action. 3\. Attempt to revisit a protected admin page. | The session ends successfully and protected admin routes require authentication again. |   |   |   |

#### *3.6.4.2 Feature-based Questionnaire* {#3.6.4.2-feature-based-questionnaire}

**Directions:** Please pick a rating for each criterion whether:

STRONGLY DISAGREE (1), DISAGREE (2), AGREE (3), or STRONGLY AGREE (4).

**Panuto:** Pumili ng antas ng iyong pagsang-ayon para sa bawat pamantayan kung ikaw ay:

LUBOS NA HINDI SUMASANG-AYON (1), HINDI SUMASANG-AYON (2), SUMASANG-AYON (3), o LUBOS NA SUMASANG-AYON (4).

**Actor: Tenant / Nangungupahan**

| Statement  | STRONGLY DISAGREE (1) | DISAGREE (2) | AGREE (3) | STRONGLY AGREE (4) |
| ----- | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| **1\.** The login and sign-up process is straightforward and allows users to access the appropriate portal based on their role.  *(Ang login at sign-up process ay straightforward at pinapayagan ang mga users na ma-access ang nararapat na portal base sa kanilang role.)* |  |  |  |  |
| **2\.** The dashboard landing page effectively routes users to the correct interface and displays a welcoming overview based on their assigned role. *(Ang dashboard landing page ay epektibong nira-route ang mga users sa tamang interface at nagdi-display ng isang welcoming overview base sa kanilang assigned role.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **3\.** The digital lease signing feature provides a clear and user-friendly process for reviewing and electronically signing lease agreements. *(Ang digital lease signing feature ay nagbibigay ng isang clear at user-friendly process para sa pag-review at electronically signing ng mga lease agreements.)* |  |  |  |  |
| **4\.** The lease overview displays all relevant information such as lease terms, start and end dates, renewal eligibility, and current status. *(Ang lease overview ay nagdi-display ng lahat ng relevant information gaya ng lease terms, start at end dates, renewal eligibility, at current status.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **5\.** The document vault provides convenient access to signed master agreements, verified reports, amendments, and other lease-related documents. *(Ang document vault ay nagbibigay ng convenient access sa mga signed master agreements, verified reports, amendments, at iba pang lease-related documents.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **6\.** The maintenance request submission feature allows tenants to easily report issues with image uploads and detailed descriptions. *(Ang maintenance request submission feature ay pinapayagan ang mga tenants na madaling mag-report ng mga issues gamit ang mga image uploads at detailed descriptions.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **7\.** The repair ticket tracking feature provides real-time status updates from pending to completed. *(Ang repair ticket tracking feature ay nagbibigay ng mga real-time status updates mula pending hanggang completed.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **8\.** The real-time messaging platform enables effective communication with the landlord through typing indicators, read receipts, and file sharing. *(Ang real-time messaging platform ay nagbibigay-daan sa epektibong komunikasyon sa landlord gamit ang mga typing indicators, read receipts, at file sharing.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **9\.** The iRis AI assistant provides accurate and helpful responses to questions about building rules, amenities, lease details, and recommendations. *(Ang iRis AI assistant ay nagbibigay ng accurate at helpful na mga responses sa mga tanong tungkol sa building rules, amenities, lease details, at recommendations.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **10\.** The financial ledger and payment history display clear breakdowns of rent, water, electricity, and other charges. *(Ang financial ledger at payment history ay nagdi-display ng malinaw na breakdowns ng rent, water, electricity, at iba pang mga charges.)* |  |  |  |  |
| **11\.** The read-only unit map offers clear visual representation of the building layout for tenant orientation. *(Ang read-only unit map ay nag-aalok ng malinaw na visual representation ng building layout para sa tenant orientation.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **12\.** The unit transfer request feature provides a straightforward process for requesting transfers to vacant units. *(Ang unit transfer request feature ay nagbibigay ng isang straightforward process para sa pag-request ng mga transfers sa mga vacant units.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **13\.** The move-out request feature allows digital submission of move-out requests with clear status tracking. *(Ang move-out request feature ay pinapayagan ang digital submission ng mga move-out requests na may kasamang malinaw na status tracking.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **14\.** The product tour and onboarding features effectively guide new tenants through the system capabilities. *(Ang product tour at onboarding features ay epektibong naggagabay sa mga bagong tenants sa mga system capabilities.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **15\.** The notification system effectively prioritizes and delivers relevant alerts about maintenance, payments, and community announcements. *(Ang notification system ay epektibong nag-pri-prioritize at nag-de-deliver ng mga relevant alerts tungkol sa maintenance, payments, at community announcements.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **16\.** The tenant application tracking with dynamic progress indicators clearly shows application timeline and required actions. *(Ang tenant application tracking na may dynamic progress indicators ay malinaw na nagpapakita ng application timeline at mga required actions.)* |  |  |  |  |
| **17\.** The account management features such as profile viewing and password changes are simple and secure. *(Ang account management features gaya ng profile viewing at password changes ay simple at secure.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **18\.** The community hub discussion post feature provides an intuitive way to start and participate in building-wide communication threads. *(Ang community hub discussion post feature ay nagbibigay ng isang intuitive na paraan upang magsimula at lumahok sa mga building-wide communication threads.)*  |  |  |  |  |

**Actor: Landlord / Nagpapaupa**

| Statement  | STRONGLY DISAGREE (1) | DISAGREE (2) | AGREE (3) | STRONGLY AGREE (4) |
| ----- | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| **1\.** The dashboard analytics with AI-driven KPI insights provide meaningful summaries of portfolio performance including earnings, occupancy, and maintenance metrics. *(Ang dashboard analytics na may AI-driven KPI insights ay nagbibigay ng mga meaningful summaries ng portfolio performance kasama na ang earnings, occupancy, at maintenance metrics.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **2\.** The simplified and detailed analytics toggle effectively balances comprehensive data with readable presentation for different user preferences. *(Ang simplified at detailed analytics toggle ay epektibong nagba-balance ng comprehensive data at readable presentation para sa iba't ibang user preferences.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **3\.** The date range filtering with presets and custom selection provides flexible reporting windows. *(Ang date range filtering na may mga presets at custom selection ay nagbibigay ng mga flexible reporting windows.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **4\.** The branded PDF report generation provides professional portfolio performance documents for auditing purposes. *(Ang branded PDF report generation ay nagbibigay ng mga professional portfolio performance documents para sa mga layunin ng auditing.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **5\.** The CSV export and export history features allow convenient data extraction and maintain a chronological log of generated reports. *(Ang CSV export at export history features ay nagbibigay-daan sa madaling data extraction at nagpapanatili ng isang chronological log ng mga generated reports.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **6\.** The unit map with drag-and-drop and grid snapping offers precise creation of property layouts. *(Ang unit map na may drag-and-drop at grid snapping ay nag-aalok ng precise creation ng mga property layouts.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **7\.** The property and unit management interface allows straightforward creation and editing of property records, unit pricing, deposits, and amenities. *(Ang property and unit management interface ay nagbibigay-daan sa straightforward na pag-create at pag-edit ng mga property records, unit pricing, deposits, at amenities.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **8\.** The walk-in application processing workflow captures sufficient information for prospective tenant evaluation including documents and checklists. *(Ang walk-in application processing workflow ay kumukuha ng sapat na impormasyon para sa prospective tenant evaluation, kabilang ang mga documents at checklists.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **9\.** The application status workflow with visual progress indicators clearly tracks application progress from pending to approved. *(Ang application status workflow na may visual progress indicators ay malinaw na nagtra-track ng application progress mula pending hanggang sa maging approved.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **10\.** The lease finalization process creates lease records from approved applications and provisions tenant accounts with clear credential distribution. *(Ang lease finalization process ay gumagawa ng mga lease records mula sa mga approved applications at nagpo-provision ng mga tenant accounts na may malinaw na credential distribution.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **11\.** The dual-mode lease signing workflow supporting both in-person and remote signing with tenant-first signing order is straightforward and secure. *(Ang dual-mode lease signing workflow na sumusuporta sa parehong in-person at remote signing na may tenant-first signing order ay straightforward at secure.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **12\.** The maintenance dashboard provides effective tracking of repair tickets with priority-based status from pending through completed. *(Ang maintenance dashboard ay nagbibigay ng epektibong pag-track ng mga repair tickets na may priority-based status mula sa pagiging pending hanggang sa ito ay maging completed.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **13\.** The maintenance ticket rich media galleries with multi-image support provide adequate context for repair assessment. *(Ang maintenance ticket rich media galleries na may multi-image support ay nagbibigay ng sapat na context para sa repair assessment.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **14\.** The invoice management feature allows creation and tracking of invoices with automatic breakdowns of base rent, water, and electricity. *(Ang invoice management feature ay nagbibigay-daan sa pag-create at pag-track ng mga invoices na may automatic breakdowns ng base rent, water, at electricity.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **15\.** The real-time messaging system with typing indicators, read receipts, and file sharing enables effective communication with tenants. *(Ang real-time messaging system na may mga typing indicators, read receipts, at file sharing ay nagbibigay-daan sa epektibong pakikipag-komunikasyon sa mga tenants.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **16\.** The tenant management features including direct messaging, one-click calling, and financial ledger access provide comprehensive tenant oversight. *(Ang tenant management features kabilang ang direct messaging, one-click calling, at financial ledger access ay nagbibigay ng isang comprehensive tenant oversight.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **17\.** The renewal eligibility engine dynamically calculates and displays appropriate renewal windows based on lease records. *(Ang renewal eligibility engine ay dinamikong nagka-calculate at nagpapakita ng mga naaangkop na renewal windows base sa mga lease records.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **18\.** The featured property analytics with total sales, views, and month-over-month growth provides effective property performance tracking. *(Ang featured property analytics na may total sales, views, at month-over-month growth ay nagbibigay ng epektibong property performance tracking.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **19\.** The account management features such as profile viewing and password changes are simple and secure. *(Ang account management features gaya ng profile viewing at password changes ay simple at secure.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **20\.** The community hub moderation dashboard with approval queue management and content reporting provides adequate tools for community governance. *(Ang community hub moderation dashboard na may approval queue management at content reporting ay nagbibigay ng sapat na mga tool para sa community governance.)*  |  |  |  |  |

**Actor: Administrator**

| Statement | STRONGLY DISAGREE (1) | DISAGREE (2) | AGREE (3) | STRONGLY AGREE (4) |
| ----- | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| **1\.** The live metrics dashboard provides real-time insights into platform health through Total Users, Properties, Leases, and Pending Reviews. *(Ang live metrics dashboard ay nagbibigay ng real-time insights sa platform health sa pamamagitan ng Total Users, Properties, Leases, at Pending Reviews.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **2\.** The registration pipeline visualization clearly tracks landlord application progress through stages from Pending to Reviewing to Approved. *(Ang registration pipeline visualization ay malinaw na nagtra-track ng landlord application progress sa pamamagitan ng mga stages mula sa Pending, patungong Reviewing, hanggang sa maging Approved.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **3\.** The user management with role-specific filter combinations and search functionality enables efficient platform-wide user governance. *(Ang user management na may role-specific filter combinations at search functionality ay nagbibigay-daan sa mahusay na platform-wide user governance.)*  |  |  |  |  |
| **4\.** The registration review modals with document and photo inspection capabilities provide sufficient information for applicant evaluation decisions. *(Ang registration review modals na may document at photo inspection capabilities ay nagbibigay ng sapat na impormasyon para sa mga applicant evaluation decisions.)*   |  |  |  |  |
| **5\.** The ability to add internal admin notes and update application status with icon-coded badges enables efficient application processing workflow. *(Ang kakayahang magdagdag ng mga internal admin notes at mag-update ng application status gamit ang mga icon-coded badges ay nagbibigay-daan sa isang efficient application processing workflow.)*   |  |  |  |  |
| **6\.** The secure sign-out mechanism with session termination ensures audit-safe account protection. *(Ang secure sign-out mechanism na may session termination ay tinitiyak ang audit-safe account protection.)*   |  |  |  |  |
| **7\.** The role-based access control enforcement through Row-Level Security effectively prevents unauthorized cross-role data access. *(Ang role-based access control (RBAC) enforcement sa pamamagitan ng Row-Level Security (RLS) ay epektibong humahadlang sa unauthorized cross-role data access.)*   |  |  |  |  |
| **8\.** The system provides adequate administrative controls and monitoring capabilities to maintain platform governance and operational oversight. *(Ang system ay nagbibigay ng sapat na administrative controls at monitoring capabilities upang mapanatili ang platform governance at operational oversight.)*   |  |  |  |  |
| **9\.** The account management features such as profile viewing and password changes are simple and secure. *(Ang account management features gaya ng profile viewing at password changes ay simple at secure.)*   |  |  |  |  |
| **10\.** The system provides adequate guidance and tutorials to support administrative responsibilities. *(Ang system ay nagbibigay ng sapat na guidance at tutorials upang suportahan ang mga administrative responsibilities.)*   |  |  |  |  |

#### *3.6.4.3 ISO-based Questionnaire* {#3.6.4.3-iso-based-questionnaire}

**Directions:** Please pick a rating for each criterion whether:

STRONGLY DISAGREE (1), DISAGREE (2), AGREE (3), or STRONGLY AGREE (4).

**Note:** This questionnaire is aligned with the current product quality model in ISO/IEC 25010:2023, which is the active edition used in 2025\.

| ISO/IEC 25010 Criteria | STRONGLY DISAGREE (1) | DISAGREE (2) | AGREE (3) | STRONGLY AGREE (4) |
| ----- | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| **A. Functional Suitability** |  |  |  |  |
| **Functional Completeness:** Degree to which the set of functions covers all the specified tasks and intended users' objectives. **As a Tenant,** *I was able to access lease information, payments, maintenance requests, messages, community posts, and other features needed for my residency transactions.* **As a Landlord,** *I was able to manage properties, units, tenants, applications, leases, invoices, maintenance concerns, and reports using the system.* **As an Administrator,** *I was able to monitor platform-wide activities, manage governance functions, and oversee user registration and system operations.* |   |   |   |   |
| **Functional Correctness:** Degree to which a product or system provides accurate results when used by intended users. **As a Tenant,** *I was able to view correct lease details, balances, payment records, and maintenance statuses.* **As a Landlord,** *I was able to obtain accurate unit, tenant, billing, and maintenance information to support my decisions and daily operations.* **As an Administrator,** *I was able to view correct platform metrics, registration statuses, and monitoring information for administrative review.* |   |   |   |   |
| **Functional Appropriateness:** Degree to which the functions facilitate the accomplishment of specified tasks and objectives. **As a Tenant,** *I was able to use the available features to complete my tasks conveniently and efficiently.* **As a Landlord,** *I was able to use the appropriate tools and controls to manage my properties and tenants efficiently.* **As an Administrator,** *I was able to use the system's administrative functions to properly govern and monitor platform activities.* |   |   |   |   |
| **B. Performance Efficiency** |  |  |  |  |
| **Time Behavior:** Degree to which the response time and throughput rates of a product or system, when performing its functions, meet requirements. **As a Tenant,** *I was able to view my lease, payments, maintenance requests, and messages without significant delays.* **As a Landlord,** *I was able to manage properties, applications, leases, and invoices with responsive system behavior.* **As an Administrator,** *I was able to review registrations, user records, and dashboards within acceptable response times.* |   |   |   |   |
| **Resource Utilization:** Degree to which the amounts and types of resources used by a product or system, when performing its functions, meet requirements. **As a Tenant,** *I was able to use the system smoothly when opening records, uploading maintenance photos, and sending messages.* **As a Landlord,** *I was able to perform management tasks and generate reports without unnecessary lag or system slowdown.* **As an Administrator,** *I was able to access monitoring tools and registration records without performance degradation.* |   |   |   |   |
| **Capacity:** Degree to which the maximum limits of a product or system parameter meet requirements. **As a Tenant,** *I was able to use the system even when other users were active at the same time.* **As a Landlord,** *I was able to manage multiple tenants, units, and records concurrently without major issues.* **As an Administrator,** *I was able to monitor simultaneous system activities and multiple user accounts without noticeable delays.* |   |   |   |   |
| **C. Compatibility** |  |  |  |  |
| **Co-existence:** Degree to which a product can perform its required functions efficiently while sharing a common environment and resources with other products, without detrimental impact on any other product. **As a Tenant,** *I was able to use the system together with other normal browser or device activities without disruption.* **As a Landlord,** *I was able to manage records, reports, and communication while the system remained stable in my working environment.* **As an Administrator,** *I was able to perform monitoring and administrative functions without the system interfering with other essential tools.* |   |   |   |   |
| **Interoperability:** Degree to which a system, product or component can exchange information with other products and mutually use the information that has been exchanged. **As a Tenant,** *I was able to upload and access files and records needed for maintenance, lease, and communication activities.* **As a Landlord,** *I was able to use uploaded documents, images, lease files, and generated reports effectively within my workflow.* **As an Administrator,** *I was able to review submitted records and administrative information from different parts of the system consistently.* |   |   |   |   |
| **D. Interaction Capability** |  |  |  |  |
| **Appropriateness Recognizability:** Degree to which users can recognize whether a product or system is appropriate for their needs. **As a Tenant,** *I was able to quickly recognize the features relevant to my lease, payments, maintenance, and communication needs.* **As a Landlord,** *I was able to identify the management tools needed for properties, tenants, leases, billing, and reports.* **As an Administrator,** *I was able to recognize the monitoring and governance tools needed for platform oversight.* |   |   |   |   |
| **Learnability:** Degree to which the functions of a product or system can be learnt to be used by specified users within a specified amount of time. **As a Tenant,** *I was able to learn how to use the system's main features within a short period of time.* **As a Landlord,** *I was able to understand and use the property management and tenant management functions without excessive training.* **As an Administrator,** *I was able to learn the dashboard, review tools, and monitoring controls efficiently.* |   |   |   |   |
| **Operability:** Degree to which a product or system has attributes that make it easy to operate and control. **As a Tenant,** *I was able to operate the system conveniently for my daily transactions.* **As a Landlord,** *I was able to operate the management tools effectively for handling records, payments, maintenance, and communication.* **As an Administrator,** *I was able to use the controls and review interfaces conveniently for administrative tasks.* |   |   |   |   |
| **User Engagement:** Degree to which a user interface presents functions and information in an inviting and motivating manner encouraging continued interaction. **As a Tenant,** *I found the interface clear and engaging while using its services and communication features.* **As a Landlord,** *I found the interface organized and suitable for regular property management work.* **As an Administrator,** *I found the dashboard and monitoring views effective and engaging for oversight tasks.* |   |   |   |   |
| **Inclusivity:** Degree to which a product or system can be used by people of various backgrounds (such as people of various ages, abilities, cultures, ethnicities, languages, genders, economic situations, etc.). **As a Tenant,** *I was able to understand and use the system regardless of my level of technical experience.* **As a Landlord,** *I was able to use the system effectively even when managing different users and situations across my properties.* **As an Administrator,** *I was able to interact with the system in a way that supports different operational contexts and user backgrounds.* |  |  |  |  |
| **User Assistance:** Degree to which a product can be used by people with the widest range of characteristics and capabilities to achieve specified goals in a specified context of use. **As a Tenant,** *I was able to rely on available guidance, prompts, and interface cues when using the system's features.* **As a Landlord,** *I was able to complete management tasks with the help of clear instructions and workflow guidance in the system.* **As an Administrator,** *I was able to use the dashboard and administrative tools with enough support from the system's instructions and prompts.* |  |  |  |  |
| **Self-descriptiveness:** Degree to wich a product presents appropriate information, where needed by the user, to make its capabilities and use immediately obvious to the user without excessive interactions with a product or other resources (such as user documentation, help desks or other users). **As a Tenant,** *I was able to understand the available features and actions through the system's labels and on-screen information.* **As a Landlord,** *I was able to understand management tools and workflows through the system's visible descriptions and controls.* **As an Administrator,** *I was able to recognize administrative functions and their purposes through the system's displayed information and labels.* |  |  |  |  |
| **E. Reliability** |  |  |  |  |
| **Faultlessness:** Degree to which a system, product or component performs specified functions without fault under normal operation. **As a Tenant,** *I was able to use the system's regular features without encountering faults that interfered with my tasks.* **As a Landlord,** *I was able to carry out routine management activities without faults that disrupted normal operations.* **As an Administrator,** *I was able to perform monitoring and review functions without faults during normal use.* |  |  |  |  |
| **Availability:** Degree to which a system, product or component is operational and accessible when required for use. **As a Tenant,** *I was able to access the system whenever I needed to check lease, payment, maintenance, or messaging records.* **As a Landlord,** *I was able to access the system whenever I needed to manage properties, tenants, and transactions.* **As an Administrator,** *I was able to access the system whenever I needed to monitor registrations and platform activities.* |   |   |   |   |
| **Fault Tolerance:** Degree to which a system, product or component operates as intended despite the presence of hardware or software faults. **As a Tenant,** *I was able to continue using essential functions despite minor issues or temporary interruptions.* **As a Landlord,** *I was able to continue management activities even when minor system problems occurred.* **As an Administrator,** *I was able to continue administrative review and monitoring despite small disruptions.* |   |   |   |   |
| **Recoverability:** Degree to which, in the event of an interruption or a failure, a product or system can recover the data directly affected and re-establish the desired state of the system. **As a Tenant,** *I observed that my essential information and records remained available after interruptions.* **As a Landlord,** *I observed that important management data and transaction records remained intact after errors or interruptions.* **As an Administrator,** *I observed that administrative records and monitoring information were preserved after disruptions.* |   |   |   |   |
| **F. Security** |  |  |  |  |
| **Confidentiality:** Degree to which a product or system ensures that data are accessible only to those authorized to have access. **As a Tenant,** *I was able to access only my own authorized records and transactions.* **As a Landlord,** *I was able to view and manage only the properties, units, tenants, and records assigned to my authority.* **As an Administrator,** *I was able to access administrative data appropriately while still observing role-based restrictions across the system.* |   |   |   |   |
| **Integrity:** Degree to which a system, product or component ensures that the state of its system and data are protected from unauthorized modification or deletion either by malicious action or computer error. **As a Tenant,** *I observed that my lease, payment, and maintenance records were protected from unauthorized changes.* **As a Landlord,** *I observed that property, tenant, billing, and lease records remained accurate and protected from improper modification.* **As an Administrator,** *I observed that governance records and user information were protected from unauthorized changes.* |   |   |   |   |
| **Non-repudiation:** Degree to which actions or events can be proven to have taken place so that the events or actions cannot be repudiated later. **As a Tenant,** *I was able to see evidence of my transactions and submitted actions within the system.* **As a Landlord,** *I was able to verify important approvals, records, and transactions that I processed through the system.* **As an Administrator,** *I was able to review evidence of administrative and operational actions recorded in the platform.* |   |   |   |   |
| **Accountability:** Degree to which the actions of an entity can be traced uniquely to the entity. **As a Tenant,** *I observed that my actions in the system were tied to my own account.* **As a Landlord,** *I observed that property management actions could be associated with the responsible user account.* **As an Administrator,** *I was able to trace important activities to the proper user or administrative account.* |   |   |   |   |
| **Authenticity:** Degree to which the identity of a subject or resource can be proved to be the one claimed. **As a Tenant,** *I felt that access to my account and records was protected by proper user verification.* **As a Landlord,** *I observed that user access and important transactions were handled through legitimate and verified accounts.* **As an Administrator,** *I was able to rely on authenticated user access and verified administrative records.* |   |   |   |   |
| **G. Maintainability** |  |  |  |  |
| **Modularity:** Degree to which a system or computer program is composed of discrete components such that a change to one component has minimal impact on other components. **As a Developer / IT Expert,** *I was able to observe that the system is divided into organized modules for authentication, property management, lease workflows, billing, maintenance, messaging, community, and administration.* |   |   |   |   |
| **Reusability:** Degree to which a product can be used as an asset in more than one system, or in building other assets. **As a Developer / IT Expert,** *I was able to identify reusable interface components, services, and logic across multiple parts of the system.* |   |   |   |   |
| **Analyzability:** Degree of effectiveness and efficiency with which it is possible to assess the impact on a product or system of an intended change to one or more of its parts, to diagnose a product for deficiencies or causes of failures, or to identify parts to be modified. **As a Developer / IT Expert,** *I was able to identify the affected modules and possible causes when examining issues or planning changes in the system.* |   |   |   |   |
| **Modifiability:** Degree to which a product or system can be effectively and efficiently modified without introducing defects or degrading existing product quality. **As a Developer / IT Expert,** *I was able to modify or improve system features with manageable effort and with minimal effect on overall stability.* |   |   |   |   |
| **Testability:** Degree of effectiveness and efficiency with which test criteria can be established for a system, product or component and tests can be performed to determine whether those criteria have been met. **As a Developer / IT Expert,** *I was able to test system modules and workflows effectively before and after integration.* |   |   |   |   |
| **H. Flexibility** |  |  |  |  |
| **Adaptability:** Degree to which a product or system can effectively and efficiently be adapted for or transferred to different hardware, software or other operational or usage environments. **As a Tenant,** *I was able to use the system effectively in the supported device and browser environment available to me.* **As a Landlord,** *I was able to manage records and transactions effectively across supported browsing environments.* **As an Administrator,** *I was able to access monitoring and review functions effectively in the supported environment.* |   |   |   |   |
| **Scalability:** Degree to which a product can handle growing or shrinking workloads or to adapt its capacity to handle variability. **As a Tenant,** *I was able to use the system properly even while many activities and records existed in the platform.* **As a Landlord,** *I was able to manage increasing numbers of units, tenants, and transactions without significant slowdown.* **As an Administrator,** *I was able to oversee growing platform activity without major loss of performance.* |   |   |   |   |
| **Installability:** Degree of effectiveness and efficiency with which a product or system can be successfully installed and/or uninstalled in a specified environment. **As a Developer / IT Expert,** *I was able to understand that the system can be deployed and maintained in its target environment with reasonable effort.* |   |   |   |   |
| **I. Safety** |  |  |  |  |
| **Operational Constraints:** Degree to which a product or system constrains its operation to within safe parameters or states when encountering operational hazard. **As a Tenant,** *I observed that the system restricted me from actions outside my authorized access.* **As a Landlord,** *I observed that the system limited actions according to my role and management authority.* **As an Administrator,** *I observed that the system applied operational restrictions appropriately across users and modules.* |   |   |   |   |
| **Risk Identification:** Degree to which a product can identify a course of events or operations that can expose life, property or environment to unacceptable risk. **As a Tenant,** *I was informed when records, requests, or transactions were incomplete or invalid.* **As a Landlord,** *I was able to identify issues such as incomplete submissions, conflicting actions, or missing requirements before finalizing important transactions.* **As an Administrator,** *I was able to detect problematic registration or monitoring conditions before making administrative decisions.* |   |   |   |   |
| **Fail-safe:** Degree to which a product can automatically place itself in a safe operating mode, or to revert to a safe condition in the event of a failure. **As a Tenant,** *I was protected by system safeguards that prevented unsafe or invalid outcomes during failures or mistakes.* **As a Landlord,** *I was able to rely on the system to maintain safe and controlled behavior during important management transactions when problems occurred.* **As an Administrator,** *I observed that the system preserved safe operating behavior during failures or risky situations.* |   |   |   |   |
| **Hazard Warnings:** Degree to which a product or system provides warnings of unacceptable risks to operations or internal controls so that they can react in sufficient time to sustain safe operations. **As a Tenant,** *I received warnings before performing actions that could affect important records or requests.* **As a Landlord,** *I received warnings before deleting, updating, rejecting, or finalizing important management transactions.* **As an Administrator,** *I received warnings before performing critical administrative actions that could affect records or users.* |   |   |   |   |

### **3.6.5 Data Categorization** {#3.6.5-data-categorization}

	The following range of weighted means and interpretations will be used to classify the responses from IT experts and end users regarding the usability, effectiveness, and overall software quality of the iReside system based on ISO/IEC 25010 quality standards.

| RANGE OF WEIGHTED MEAN | INTERPRETATION |
| :---: | ----- |
| 3.25 \- 4.00 | Strongly Agree |
| 2.50 \- 3.24 | Agree |
| 1.75 \- 2.49 | Disagree |
| 1.00 \- 1.74 | Strongly Disagree |

STRONGLY AGREE (4): The respondents strongly agree that the iReside system fully satisfies the specified ISO/IEC 25010 quality characteristic, demonstrating excellent performance and a high degree of acceptability.

AGREE (3): The respondents agree that the iReside system adequately satisfies the specified ISO/IEC 25010 quality characteristic, demonstrating satisfactory performance and acceptability.

DISAGREE (2): The respondents disagree that the iReside system satisfies the specified ISO/IEC 25010 quality characteristic, indicating that improvement is needed in that aspect of the system.

STRONGLY DISAGREE (1): The respondents strongly disagree that the iReside system satisfies the specified ISO/IEC 25010 quality characteristic, indicating critical inadequacy and the need for major improvement.
